title: In The Wilderness
content:
Christ of His gentleness
    Thirsting and hungering,
    Walked in the wilderness;
    Soft words of grace He spoke
    Unto lost desert-folk
    That listened wondering.
    He heard the bitterns call
    From ruined palace-wall,
    Answered them brotherly.
    He held communion
    With the she-pelican
    Of lonely piety.
    Basilisk, cockatrice,
    Flocked to his homilies,
    With mail of dread device,
    With monstrous barb�d slings,
    With eager dragon-eyes;
    Great rats on leather wings
    And poor blind broken things,
    Foul in their miseries.
    And ever with Him went,
    Of all His wanderings
    Comrade, with ragged coat,
    Gaunt ribs, poor innocent, 
    Bleeding foot, burning throat,
    The guileless old scapegoat;
    For forty nights and days
    Followed in Jesus' ways,
    Sure guard behind Him kept,
    Tears like a lover wept.
meaning:
Robert von Ranke Graves's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "In The Wilderness"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
